sections.title: Setup gap (blocking everything else)
sections.content:
No test runner is installed — `npm test` only runs the content-integrity script. Add:

```bash
npm i -D vitest jsdom @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom
```

```ts
// vite.config.ts — add test block
test: { environment: 'jsdom', globals: true }
```

---

sections.title: `src/lib/content.ts`
1. `isBook` (unexported validator) — no coverage of rejection paths
2. `countWords` — edge cases untested
sections.content:
```ts
describe('countWords', () => {
  it('returns 0 for empty string', () => expect(countWords('')).toBe(0))
  it('returns 0 for whitespace-only', () => expect(countWords('   \n\t ')).toBe(0))
  it('collapses multiple spaces/newlines between words', () =>
    expect(countWords('a   b\n\nc')).toBe(3))
})
```

sections.title: 3. `getBook` — undefined / unknown id path never tested
4. `booksInCategory` — empty-result and multi-match paths untested
5. `loadChapter` — the most logic-heavy function, zero coverage
6. `bookStats` — savedPct clamping untested
7. `fmtDuration` — no tests at all
sections.content:
```ts
describe('fmtDuration', () => {
  it('formats < 60 minutes as "N min"', () => expect(fmtDuration(45)).toBe('45 min'))
  it('formats exact hours without minutes remainder', () => expect(fmtDuration(120)).toBe('2 h'))
  it('formats hours + minutes', () => expect(fmtDuration(125)).toBe('2 h 5 m'))
  it('handles 0 minutes', () => expect(fmtDuration(0)).toBe('0 min'))
})
```

sections.title: 8. `searchBooks` — ranking/scoring logic entirely untested
`src/lib/progress.ts`
9. `sanitize` (unexported) — corrupted-localStorage recovery, the whole point of this function, is untested
sections.content:
```ts
describe('useBookProgress / sanitize (via localStorage fixtures)', () => {
  it('recovers from a non-object stored value', () => { /* localStorage set to "null", "123", "[]" */ })
  it('dedupes and sorts chaptersRead', () => { /* stored [3,1,1,2] → [1,2,3] */ })
  it('drops non-number entries from chaptersRead', () => { /* stored [1,"x",2] → [1,2] */ })
  it('defaults mapRead to false for any non-true value', () => {})
})
```

sections.title: 10. `markChapterRead` / `toggleChapterRead` — idempotency and toggle-off untested
11. `completionPct` — no tests, includes a clamp (`Math.min(read, totalChapters)`) that guards against corrupted data
12. `latestProgress` — untested, including the "no progress yet" path
sections.content:
```ts
describe('latestProgress', () => {
  it('returns null when no book has updatedAt > 0', () => expect(latestProgress({})).toBeNull())
  it('picks the book with the highest updatedAt', () => { /* two books, differing updatedAt */ })
})
```

---

sections.title: `src/lib/prefs.ts`
13. `bumpFontScale` — clamp boundaries never verified
sections.content:
```ts
describe('bumpFontScale', () => {
  it('clamps to 1.3 max', () => { updatePrefs({ fontScale: 1.3 }); bumpFontScale(0.5); /* expect 1.3 */ })
  it('clamps to 0.85 min', () => { updatePrefs({ fontScale: 0.85 }); bumpFontScale(-0.5); /* expect 0.85 */ })
  it('rounds to 2 decimal places', () => { /* avoid float drift like 1.0500000001 */ })
})
```

sections.title: 14. `resolveReaderTheme` — fallback chain untested
sections.content:
```ts
describe('resolveReaderTheme', () => {
  it('uses explicit readerTheme when set', () => expect(resolveReaderTheme({ readerTheme: 'sepia', appTheme: 'dark', fontScale: 1, wide: false })).toBe('sepia'))
  it('falls back to dark when readerTheme is null and appTheme is dark', () => expect(resolveReaderTheme({ readerTheme: null, appTheme: 'dark', fontScale: 1, wide: false })).toBe('dark'))
  it('falls back to paper when readerTheme is null and appTheme is light', () => expect(resolveReaderTheme({ readerTheme: null, appTheme: 'light', fontScale: 1, wide: false })).toBe('paper'))
})
```

---

sections.title: `src/lib/store.ts`
15. `createLocalStore` — error-handling paths are the entire reason this wrapper exists, and are untested
`src/lib/markdown.tsx`
16. `mdToHtml` / `mdInline` — untested, plus a security note
sections.content:
```ts
describe('mdToHtml', () => {
  it('renders GFM tables/strikethrough (gfm: true)', () => { /* ~~x~~ → <del>x</del> */ })
})
```
**Security note (not just a coverage gap):** `Markdown` renders via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` with no sanitizer (no DOMPurify). It's currently safe only because all Markdown comes from trusted local `.md` files. If chapter content is ever sourced from user input or a CMS, this becomes an XSS vector — worth a regression test pinning "content source stays trusted local files" if that assumption is load-bearing.

---

sections.title: Integration/component gaps (all currently 0%)
sections.content:
- **`Reader.tsx`**: keyboard navigation (`onKey` handler), scroll-progress tracking, `finishChapter` marking progress — no test drives a keypress or scroll event through to `markChapterRead`.
- **`Library.tsx`**: search-input → `useMemo(searchBooks)` wiring, keyboard shortcut to focus search, `ContinueStrip` rendering when `latestProgress` is null vs populated.
- **`BookMap.tsx`**: `MapChapterCard` rendering against real book fixtures.
- **`App.tsx`**: routing (unknown route → redirect/404 behavior untested).

```tsx
// tests/pages/Reader.test.tsx (example integration skeleton)
import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react'
import { MemoryRouter } from 'react-router-dom'

it('marks a chapter as read when the user reaches the end and navigates away', async () => {
  render(<MemoryRouter initialEntries={['/book/some-id/chapter/1']}><App /></MemoryRouter>)
  // simulate scroll to bottom, then navigation; assert localStorage/progress store updated
})
```

---

**Priority order**: `content.ts` (`loadChapter`, `searchBooks`, `bookStats`) and `progress.ts` (`sanitize`, `completionPct`) first — they're pure functions with real branching logic and no framework needed to test. `store.ts` error paths second — they're the app's only defense against corrupted/unavailable localStorage. Component/integration tests last, once Testing Library is wired up.

// vite.config.ts — add test block
test: { environment: 'jsdom', globals: true }
